oligos.name: Smartseq3_Oligo-dT30VN
oligos.notes: 5'-biotin (/5Biosg/). 5' handle ACGAGCATCAGCAGCATACGA is the reverse-PCR-primer binding site, followed by 30 dT and an anchoring VN. 100 uM stock; used at 0.5 uM in lysis. Priming across the polyA tail.

oligos.name: Smartseq3_N8_TSO
oligos.notes: 5'-biotin (/5Biosg/). rGrGrG (three riboguanosines, folded to GGG) hybridize to the non-templated C overhang at the cDNA 5' end. AGAGACAG = terminal partial Tn5 mosaic end; ATTGCGCAATG = the 11-bp tag used to distinguish 5' UMI reads (zUMIs find_pattern); NNNNNNNN = 8-bp UMI. Used at 2 uM in RT.

oligos.name: Fwd_PCR_primer (Smartseq3 forward PCR primer)
oligos.notes: TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG = Nextera Read 1 / Tn5 mosaic-end sequence; ATTGCGCAATG = Smart-seq3 tag. Anneals to the TSO-derived 5' end. Used 0.5 uM (protocol) / 0.1 uM (Methods). Higher forward-primer concentration outcompetes residual TSO priming in PCR.

oligos.name: Rev_PCR_primer (Smartseq3 reverse PCR primer)
oligos.notes: Identical to the oligo-dT 5' handle; anneals to the reverse complement handle on the first-strand cDNA. Used 0.1 uM.

oligos.name: Custom S5XX Nextera index primer (i5)
oligos.role: Indexing PCR primer / P5 flow-cell + i5 index
oligos.notes: Standard Nextera i5 index-primer architecture used by the protocol: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC (P5) + 8- or 10-bp i5 index + TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG (Read1/ME). Custom-designed with 8/10-bp indexes, min Levenshtein distance 2-3; avoid i5 indexes starting 'AC' on NovaSeq. Exact index sequences not printed in the documents.

oligos.name: Custom N7XX Nextera index primer (i7)
oligos.role: Indexing PCR primer / P7 flow-cell + i7 index
oligos.notes: Standard Nextera i7 index-primer architecture used by the protocol: CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT (P7) + 8- or 10-bp i7 index + GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG (Read2/ME). Custom-designed indexes; exact index sequences not printed in the documents.

oligos.name: Tn5 mosaic end (ME, Nextera/ATM/TDE1)
oligos.notes: Delivered by Nextera XT ATM or Illumina TDE1 Tn5. Introduces near-random cuts and appends the ME to fragment ends for index PCR.

final_library.source_label: Smart-seq3 (Hagemann-Jensen et al., Nat Biotechnol 2020) — 5' UMI-containing fragment
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: 5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC [i5:8] TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG ATTGCGCAATG [UMI:8] GGG [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT [i7:8] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
annotation_lines: [SAMPLE_INDEX:8] — i5 sample index (custom Nextera, 8 or 10 bp; read as I2)
TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG — Nextera Read 1 primer / partial Tn5 mosaic end (forward PCR primer region; Read 1 sequencing primer binding site)
ATTGCGCAATG — 11-bp Smart-seq3 tag (marks 5' UMI-containing reads; zUMIs find_pattern)
[CDNA] — transcript insert (5' end of the RNA molecule)
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT — reverse-complement Tn5 mosaic end (added by tagmentation at the 3' break)
[SAMPLE_INDEX:8] — i7 sample index (custom Nextera; read as I1)
NOTE: Internal (non-UMI) fragments have the standard Nextera structure P5–i5–[ME]–cDNA–[ME rc]–i7–P7 with no tag/UMI/GGG, and are sequenced from either strand across the full gene body.

evidence.locator: Smart-seq3 (Hagemann-Jensen et al., Nat Biotechnol 2020) — 5' UMI-containing fragment

library_generation.title: Cell lysis / RNA denaturation
library_generation.summary: A single FACS-sorted cell is lysed in PEG/Triton buffer containing oligo-dT primer, dNTPs and RNase inhibitor, and heated to release and denature polyA+ RNA.
library_generation.note: Cells are sorted into 3 uL lysis buffer, spun and heated at 72 C for 10 min then held at 4 C. Lysis buffer already contains the Smartseq3 oligo-dT30VN primer and 5% PEG (molecular crowding).

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription + template switching
library_generation.summary: Maxima H-minus RT extends the oligo-dT primer to first-strand cDNA, adds non-templated Cs at the 5' cap, and template-switches onto the TSO's rGrGrG, appending the tag+UMI to the cDNA 3' end.
library_generation.note: RT/TS at 42 C 90 min, 10x (50 C 2 min / 42 C 2 min), 85 C 5 min. NaCl (not KCl), 5% PEG and 1 mM GTP enhance RT/TS. The TSO copy places tag ATTGCGCAATG + 8-bp UMI + GGG at the 5' end of the eventual sense strand.
library_generation.product:
oligo-dT30VN (5'-biotin)
 mRNA 5'- [CDNA sense] AAAAAAAAAA          -3'
                    <-------------------- RT
 cDNA 3'- GGG [UMI] GTAACGCGTTAGACAGAGA...(tag) [CDNA antisense] TTTTTTTTTT ACGAUACGACGACGACUACGAGCA-5'
         ^^^ template switch onto TSO rGrGrG

library_generation.title: PCR pre-amplification
library_generation.summary: The forward primer (Read1/ME + tag) and reverse primer (oligo-dT handle) amplify full-length cDNA with KAPA HiFi polymerase.
library_generation.note: 3 min 98 C; 18-25 cycles (20 s 98 C / 30 s 65 C / 4-6 min 72 C); 5 min 72 C. Excess forward primer suppresses residual TSO priming.
library_generation.product:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG ATTGCGCAATG [UMI:8] GGG [CDNA] AAAA...AAAA TCGTATGCTGCTGATGCTCGT -3'
3'- AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC TAACGCGTTAC [UMI] CCC [CDNA] TTTT...TTTT AGCATACGACGACTACGAGCA -5'
      (Fwd_PCR_primer)                                                        (Rev_PCR_primer)

library_generation.title: cDNA purification & normalization
library_generation.summary: Pre-amplified cDNA is cleaned with 22% PEG/SPRI beads, quantified and normalized to ~100 pg/uL.
library_generation.note: 0.6:1 bead ratio; optional QC on Bioanalyzer HS DNA. Normalization sets the cDNA:Tn5 ratio for tagmentation.

library_generation.title: Tn5 tagmentation
library_generation.summary: Nextera Tn5 (ATM/TDE1) makes near-random cuts in the amplified cDNA and appends mosaic-end adapters, then is stripped with SDS.
library_generation.note: 55 C 10 min, then 0.5 uL 0.2% SDS. Produces 5' UMI-tagged fragments (retaining tag+UMI end) and internal fragments spanning the gene body. Tn5:cDNA ratio tunes UMI-to-internal read fraction.
library_generation.product:
5' UMI fragment:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAGATTGCGCAATG[UMI:8]GGG[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT -3'
                                                                        ^ ME added by Tn5
internal fragment:
5'- AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT -3'

library_generation.title: Indexing PCR (Nextera i5/i7)
library_generation.summary: Custom Nextera S5xx (i5) and N7xx (i7) index primers add sample barcodes and the P5/P7 flow-cell adapters by gap-fill + PCR.
library_generation.note: 72 C 3 min gap-fill; 98 C 3 min; 12x (10 s 98 C / 30 s 55 C / 30 s 72 C); 5 min 72 C. Phusion polymerase. 8- or 10-bp custom indexes.

library_generation.title: Library clean-up & (optional) size selection
library_generation.summary: Pooled tagmented libraries are bead-purified (0.6:1) and optionally gel size-selected to ~550 bp–2 kb before quantification and sequencing.
library_generation.note: Final median size ~1 kb; quantified by Qubit/Bioanalyzer. Sequenced single- or paired-end on Illumina.

library_sequencing.primer: Read 1 primer TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG (= 5' region of the Fwd PCR primer / Nextera Read 1)
library_sequencing.note: For 5' UMI fragments R1 reads the 11-bp tag ATTGCGCAATG (bp 1-11), the 8-bp UMI (bp 12-19), the GGG (bp 20-22), then the transcript 5' end from bp 23 (zUMIs UMI(12-19), cDNA(23-150)). Reads lacking the tag are internal (non-UMI) reads spanning the gene body. Often run 75/50 bp single-end or 150 bp paired-end.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer
      TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG------------->  reads tag+UMI+GGG+cDNA
5'-...TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAGATTGCGCAATGNNNNNNNNGGG[cDNA]...-3'  top (read out)
3'-...AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTCTAACGCGTTACNNNNNNNNCCC[cDNA]...-5'  bottom (template)
      |________ Read1 / ME _____________||__ tag ___||_UMI:8_||GGG|

library_sequencing.note: 8-bp (or 10-bp) custom Nextera i7 sample index; base-mask I8. Read off the i7 side of the tagmented (3') end.
8-bp (or 10-bp) custom Nextera i5 sample index; base-mask I8. Avoid i5 indexes starting 'AC' on NovaSeq (documented artifact).

library_sequencing.primer: Read 2 primer GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG (Nextera Read 2 / i7-side ME)
library_sequencing.note: Paired-end read primed at the tagmented 3' end, reading the gene body from the opposite direction (zUMIs cDNA(1-150)); paired with R1 for in-silico molecule reconstruction. Omitted on single-end runs.

title: Smart-seq3
description: Smart-seq3 is a plate-based, full-length single-cell RNA-seq method that adds a 5′ unique molecular identifier (UMI) counting strategy to Smart-seq2 chemistry. PolyA+ RNA is reverse transcribed with an oligo-dT primer and Maxima H-minus RT, template-switched onto a TSO carrying a partial Tn5 motif, an 11-bp tag, an 8-bp UMI and three riboguanosines, then PCR pre-amplified and Tn5-tagmented. Sequencing yields strand-specific 5′ UMI-containing reads (tag + UMI + transcript 5′ end) plus internal reads spanning the full gene body, enabling molecule counting together with in-silico reconstruction of RNA molecules at allele and isoform resolution.

original_publication.title: Single-cell RNA counting at allele and isoform resolution using Smart-seq3

authors.name: Michael Hagemann-Jensen
authors.affiliation: Department of Cell and Molecular Biology, Karolinska Institutet, Stockholm, Sweden

authors.name: Anton J. M. Larsson

authors.name: Omid R. Faridani

authors.name: Rickard Sandberg
authors.affiliation: Department of Cell and Molecular Biology & Integrated Cardio Metabolic Center, Karolinska Institutet, Stockholm, Sweden

throughput.summary: Plate-based (96/384-well) with moderate cellular throughput; ~€0.5–€1 per sequencing-ready cell library. Up to ~150,000 unique molecules detected per HEK293FT cell; on average 8,710 molecules reconstructed to ≥500 bp per fibroblast. 5,376 HCA benchmark cells profiled.
throughput.cells: Hundreds to thousands per experiment (e.g. 369 mouse fibroblasts, 5,376 HCA cells); one cell per well
throughput.rna: Full-length polyA+ mRNA; 60% of counted molecules assignable to allelic origin, 30–50% to specific isoforms
throughput.dna: cDNA normalized to 100–200 pg/uL; final library median ~1 kb

publication.statistical_model: Poisson (Poisson sampling noise of incomplete RNA molecule capture in scRNA-seq); UMI collapsing by Hamming distance; chi-squared / Kruskal–Wallis tests with Benjamini–Hochberg correction for isoform analyses

other.label: Validation
other.value: In-silico molecule reconstruction validated by PacBio long-read sequencing and engineered DIY RNA spike-ins

data_processing.summary: After sequencing, BCL files are converted to FASTQ with bcl2fastq (base-mask Y150N,I8,I8,Y150N; no sample sheet / per-cell demultiplexing needed at this stage), then the zUMIs pipeline processes both the 5' UMI-containing reads and the internal reads — it flags UMI reads by the 11-bp ATTGCGCAATG tag, extracts the UMI, aligns cDNA with STAR while clipping the 3' Tn5 adapter, and collapses UMIs to count molecules.
